tweets.text: During today’s telephonic briefing, @StateDeputySpox discussed @SecBlinken’s upcoming travel to London and Kyiv, U.S. COVID-19 assistance for India, and press freedom in Venezuela. Read more. https://t.co/gms1EN4v8L

In the first 100 days, the United States rejoined the Paris Agreement and launched a global diplomatic campaign to promote ambitious emissions-reduction targets and innovative adaptation efforts from the world’s largest economies at the #LeadersClimateSummit. https://t.co/8C1XV1Yl2W

Standing up for democracy and human rights, @POTUS reaffirmed U.S. commitment to the human rights of LGBTQI+ persons around the world and joined the UN Human Rights Council as an observer. https://t.co/NgR5nDV1U5

It's International #JazzDay! We have long recognized the role that music, particularly jazz, has played in diplomacy and connecting people, especially when the world has needed it most. Learn more about music diplomacy at: https://t.co/h4cztW5BQT. https://t.co/548DqlnmHs

Ending the pandemic and building back better global health security is a priority of @POTUS's Administration. In the first 100 days, the United States reengaged the @WHO and contributed an initial $2B to COVAX to provide COVID-19 vaccines for 92 economies. https://t.co/tNjA05LAkA

As we bring #ArabAmericanHeritageMonth to a close, Acting Assistant Secretary for @StateDept_NEA, reflects on the integral contributions of Arab Americans to the fabric of U.S. society. Read more in this DipNote blog: https://t.co/2avZNjuU4X.

During National Arab American Heritage Month, we celebrate the diverse cultures and traditions of the Arab American community. The U.S. is home to millions of Arab Americans whose contributions to our country are as old as America itself. #AAHM https://t.co/jGtZqSLVay

In the first 100 Days of Diplomacy, @SecBlinken appointed the State Department’s first Chief Diversity and Inclusion Officer and announced the full resumption of training and development on diversity, equity, and inclusion. https://t.co/HcOgycOn9w

.@POTUS: No one nation can deal with all the crises of our time alone – from terrorism to nuclear proliferation to mass migration, cybersecurity, climate change – and as we’re experiencing now, the pandemic. https://t.co/3a7SBKXP5I

In the first 100 Days of Diplomacy, @SecBlinken revitalized our alliances and partnerships through robust engagement, traveled to Japan and South Korea to meet with our treaty allies, and pioneered virtual diplomacy through trips to Canada, Mexico, Central America, and Africa. https://t.co/i9uTGj6GHd

American diplomacy is strengthening alliances and partnerships, championing human rights and democracy, tackling the climate crisis, supporting global efforts to end the COVID-19 pandemic, and restoring America's leadership in the world. https://t.co/iAoXraMQUT

The United States is sending shipments of critical COVID-19 assistance to India, including oxygen equipment, PPE, testing kits, medicine, and essential supplies that will save lives. Our support will continue as the people of India fight this pandemic. https://t.co/K0PPZrAOwy https://t.co/FtDLFlbd12

.@StateDeptSpox: @SecBlinken and other officials have called on the Russian government to reconsider its actions against @RFERL and we have been gratified to hear statements from some of our international partners joining that call. https://t.co/2lFHO5LZX1

.@StateDeptSpox: We will continue to support Ukraine in strengthening its institutions, including advancing democratic institutions and corporate governance reforms, but Ukraine’s leaders must do their part. https://t.co/LpqizGqsq6

.@StateDeptSpox: "The U.S. is delivering supplies worth more than $100 million in the coming days to provide urgent relief to our partners in India. The United States is determined to help India in its time of need." Learn more: https://t.co/K0PPZrAOwy. https://t.co/4m3pg6qKsA
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/HDqimaNei3

Today at approximately 2:30 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/gUwG1OT3iq. https://t.co/EWeS5IIFjk
.@POTUS on COVID-19: We're going to fight this virus with equity - equity for all. https://t.co/KntQGUMnNh

The United States in March provided $2 billion to Gavi in support of COVAX's efforts to equitably deliver safe and effective vaccines across the globe. We call on other global leaders and the private sector to join us in supporting COVAX. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/kMEv9496gm

Together with our partners, the U.S. will reform & strengthen the institutions that safeguard global health security. This includes greater international transparency and finding a sustainable approach to prevent future pandemics. https://t.co/lt8wvGDAnn

The @Gavi COVAX Advance Market Commitment Investment Opportunity aims to raise an additional $2 billion to support vaccination of nearly 30% of populations in 92 low- and middle-income economies. Learn more: https://t.co/M8pMeT7RDt. #VaccinesWork

We are partnering with agencies and organizations across the U.S. public and private sectors to rapidly deploy assistance to India. Recently, COVID Coordinator Gayle Smith, @WHNSC, and @USAID briefed industry leaders on how they can support relief efforts. https://t.co/l8ouXGTXz2

The United States stands in solidarity with India as it battles a new wave of COVID-19 cases. We are working around the clock to rapidly deliver $100 million worth of supplies to provide urgent relief to our partners in India. Learn more below. https://t.co/K0PPZrAOwy

Our efforts to help the global COVID-19 response are about saving lives. We won't trade shots in arms for political favors. All countries, regardless of income status, need vaccines that meet rigorous standards for safety and efficacy. https://t.co/Dkd6WZopzg

Today, we honor the life of Michael Collins. He dedicated his life to service, including as a @NASA astronaut and here at the Department, where he served as Assistant Secretary of State for Public Affairs. @AstroMCollins's legacy of space diplomacy will be remembered. https://t.co/TiehbRYXY0
.@SecBlinken: This pandemic won’t end at home until it ends worldwide. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/CPxscnXroo
Live now! @SecBlinken participates in a virtual roundtable with foreign media at the @ForeignPressCtr, ahead of World Press Freedom Day. https://t.co/y4MC3OeHXW

Today at approximately 1:30 p.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will participate in a virtual roundtable with foreign media at the @ForeignPressCtr ahead of World Press Freedom Day. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/rhQDnPUToV.

.@SecBlinken appeared on @TheLeadCNN and discussed COVAX, the global vaccine response, the Khashoggi Ban, U.S.-Russian relations, Afghanistan, and restoring the refugee program. Read more: https://t.co/EshjmcxKEZ.
Addressing the root causes of migration means breaking the cycles of desperation and providing hope at home for individuals and families in North and Central America. https://t.co/Zeine6XFw0

On April 15, the United States and @Gavi hosted the virtual event, “One World Protected,” that garnered more than $300 million in pledges from government and private sector partners to support global COVID-19 vaccine access. https://t.co/vSSP80xgjp  #VaccinesWork https://t.co/l6k7W0gLub

.@SecBlinken: The world has to come together to bring the COVID pandemic to an end everywhere. And for that to happen, the United States must act and we must lead. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/hA5GCxg218

After bringing America back into the Paris Agreement, @POTUS convened the #LeadersClimateSummit last week. Learn more about how the U.S. and other countries are committed to accelerating the transition to a clean energy future at https://t.co/v7eOm4TpKr. https://t.co/frOAXuu6ga

Kenya is a critical security partner for the United States, especially in the fight against al-Shabaab. The U.S. is working with Kenya to strengthen the rule of law, border security coordination, community engagement, and terrorism response. https://t.co/DjAWaDDSWR
The US and Kenya have vibrant economic ties  - almost $1 billion in bilateral trade in 2020, creating jobs in both countries. https://t.co/3z1bAkwGCW

The United States and Nigeria are collaborating closely to combat COVID-19 and other diseases. Ongoing U.S. health programs reach more than 60 million Nigerians with lifesaving services, including by training public health workers and improving access to medicines. https://t.co/qw5FQlBhHS
The United States provided more than 9 million teacher’s guides and books in five of Nigeria’s most widely spoken languages to advance early grade reading. https://t.co/Jg9Kn8zMke

The U.S. has provided nearly $106 million for refugee protection and humanitarian assistance to help Kenya as it provides regional leadership by hosting refugees and asylum seekers from Somalia, South Sudan, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, and elsewhere. https://t.co/1AtyP13E6l

The United States and Kenya have collaborated closely to combat the COVID-19 pandemic. To date, the United States has provided more than $23 million in overall health and humanitarian assistance to Kenya to combat the pandemic and lessen economic impacts. https://t.co/dbdp0hDtZJ

The United States promotes strong and broad collaboration between government and civil society in Nigeria at all levels, including civil society organizations led by women and members of marginalized groups. https://t.co/6AZnvEwMgB
Today @SecBlinken met with members of the @YALINetwork. Read our latest DipNote on who they are and how they have contributed to their community over the past 10 years. https://t.co/TTqoaP7vH1

Live Now! Watch @SecBlinken travel virtually to Kenya and Nigeria, including an event to recognize the work of the @YALINetwork and meetings with senior officials, entrepreneurs, and healthcare workers.  https://t.co/4pD9H5ps4F

FACT: The @YALINetwork is a virtual community of more than 700,000 members across Sub-Saharan Africa, providing invaluable opportunities to connect with other leaders in their community and to learn from experts in their field. 🌍 https://t.co/36WhSxCpdL
https://t.co/8htuRTCySU

Today, @SecBlinken will travel virtually to Kenya and Nigeria, and meet with members of @YALINetwork. Follow along on his trip here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/VZVlX3OWa3. See the schedule of events below. https://t.co/ycof6a67jo

.@StateDeptSpox: We have always stood in solidarity with our friends and our partners in India. We are working non-stop across the government to do all we can to deliver the supplies most needed within India. https://t.co/mSeGWcgK0y

The United States is providing more than $11 billion for the global COVID-19 response. This money will help support broad and equitable global vaccine access, mitigate secondary impacts of COVID, and boost international pandemic preparedness. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/GwSpmu2t84

.@VP to the @UN Dialogue on Pandemic Preparedness and Response: What we do now will determine our collective future. As the world works to address this pandemic, we must also prepare for the future. We must be ready, resilient, and responsive.

.@POTUS: “Meeting the moment on climate change must begin with a recognition that every nation has a responsibility and every nation is at risk.” More: https://t.co/7Igaq1rnnq. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/5g0MzraCMt

Acting @AsstSecStateAF Robert Godec previews @SecBlinken’s virtual trip to Kenya and Nigeria tomorrow. The Secretary will host a discussion with @YALINetwork, meet with senior officials, and attend virtual events with healthcare workers and clean energy entrepreneurs. https://t.co/3iAmm74kis

.@StateDeptSpox: We have always stood in solidarity with our friends and our partners in India. We are working non-stop across the government to do all we can to deliver the supplies most needed within India. https://t.co/LPqlxEvJFn

.@StateDeptSpox remembers human rights activist Xulhaz Mannan, who was murdered five years ago for his courageous work on behalf of marginalized communities in Bangladesh. Read @SecBlinken's full statement here: https://t.co/uZ0rctC9Us. https://t.co/gAAtfz4YZd
Live now! @StateDeptSpox Ned Price briefs reporters from the U.S. Department of State. https://t.co/E7E3KvONa1

Today at approximately 3:00 p.m. EDT, @StateDeptSpox Ned Price will host a press briefing from the U.S. Department of State. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/3zsAgLMAJu. https://t.co/LY9G6QHYBi

.@POTUS: Our shared goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year in developing countries is critical. To help meet that goal, the United States will double by 2024, our annual public climate financing to developing countries. https://t.co/7Igaq1rnnq. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/81ZOXZM5oh

.@VP Harris on climate action: As a global community, it is imperative that we act quickly and together to confront this crisis. And this will require innovation and collaboration around the world. https://t.co/pMtVdWLjGc #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/3csicd0MJk
.@POTUS: This year, on #WorldIPDay, we celebrate the innovators and creators who enrich our lives and create the products, services, companies, and industries of tomorrow. https://t.co/VUz7smXEIb

This World Immunization Week, we reflect on global efforts to address preventable diseases, including COVID-19, through vaccines. Learn how the United States is working with its partners to make COVID-19 vaccines available at home & abroad: https://t.co/M8pMeSQgLV. #VaccinesWork

On #WorldIPDay, we celebrate the ingenuity and creativity behind every small and medium-sized enterprise, their courage to make a difference, and their contributions to enhancing our lives. More: https://t.co/gyI5QRHLvK. https://t.co/c3jMlxzMx6
Addressing the root causes of migration means breaking the cycles of desperation and providing hope at home for individuals and families in North and Central America. https://t.co/L9muTdandZ
.@SecBlinken: We’re working comprehensively to make sure that we have a safe, an orderly, a secure, and humane border, particularly when it comes to migration issues. https://t.co/VVBOb6KaMD
The goal of the United States is to help create conditions where the people of El Salvador, Guatemala, and Honduras can thrive. https://t.co/jrKTDzL2lA
The U.S. is providing safe, legal, and humane ways to migrate to the United States to promote prosperity throughout North and Central America.

.@POTUS: We need to work together, once again, to build a clean energy future that delivers good jobs and overcomes the threat of climate change — investing in innovation and in our people, raising our ambitions, ensuring every nation does its part. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/dnpuxyqVlA
For the last 60 years, every decade has been hotter than the one that came before it. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/v7eOm4TpKr https://t.co/XFxgOLeoOH

.@SecBlinken: As we take concrete actions to reduce emissions and prepare for the unavoidable impacts of climate change, we have an opportunity. We’re rooting for every country, every business, every community around the world to succeed in this effort. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/GY67ICm3PS
The 2020 wildfire season burned more than 10 million acres. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. https://t.co/FSKkBrcj58 #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/lKhL0Dn965

On #WorldMalariaDay, we recognize those working to #EndMalaria, including @PMIgov's efforts in sub-Saharan Africa and the Mekong. Together, we can end this deadly disease through cost-effective, lifesaving interventions. Learn more: https://t.co/hHff0UXnfp. https://t.co/wxaGtyPxZI

.@POTUS: This is a moment for all of us to build better economies for our children, our grandchildren, and all of us to thrive — to thrive in — not just now, but beyond for the next generations. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/aQfbCExGhK

.@POTUS: Nations that work together to invest in a cleaner economy will reap rewards for their citizens. The U.S. is committed — we are committed to making those investments to grow our economy here at home while connecting with markets around the world. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/5x2SMFbwfN

With $2B donated & plans to provide another $2B through 2022, the U.S. is the largest single donor to the global vaccine initiative COVAX. We encourage other donors & partners to urgently fulfill their pledges & support vaccine manufacturing, supply & distribution. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/05waSLKc2v

Immunization is one of the most cost-effective public health interventions to stop the spread of preventable infectious diseases. Learn how @USAID's support for immunization contributes to our global health and broader development objectives. https://t.co/En3txVPGho #VaccinesWork

This World Immunization Week, in the Year of the Health and Care Worker, the United States celebrates the health workers that carry vaccines through the last mile, often in challenging circumstances, to ensure all communities have access to these life-saving tools. #VaccinesWork https://t.co/YaH4MaJyyd

Have you read the latest on our official blog, DipNote? Recent entries spotlight the Greening Diplomacy Initiative, what it means to be a creator and innovator, perspectives on poetry and diplomacy, and a diplomat's story for Arab American Heritage Month. https://t.co/87RfebxJt9

2020 was the second-warmest year on record based on @NOAA’s temperature data. Land areas were hottest on record. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/v7eOm4TpKr https://t.co/AM6x8YQx3l

.@SecBlinken: If we work together, we can do more than just address this crisis. We can turn it into an opportunity to improve our societies and deliver for people worldwide, and we can lay the foundation for cooperation on other shared challenges. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/n05S62p91Q

From 1900 to 1980, a new temperature record was set on average every 13.5 years; from 1981–2019, a new record was set every 3 years. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/v7eOm4TpKr https://t.co/b6carX8tkM

.@POTUS on the climate crisis: We have to move. We have to move quickly to meet these challenges. The steps our countries take between now and Glasgow will set the world up for success to protect livelihoods around the world and keep global warming at a maximum of 1.5° Celsius. https://t.co/P860tVkzeq

.@POTUS: The countries that take decisive action now to create the industries of the future will be the ones that reap the economic benefits of the clean energy boom that’s coming. https://t.co/sHi4prhIYG

The United States is fully committed to multilateral cooperation, strengthening our alliances and partnerships, and leading with diplomacy to address the shared global challenges of today and the future. #DiplomacyDay https://t.co/mFp8M7WYTH

Since satellite-based measurements began in the late 1970s, Arctic sea ice extent has decreased in all months and virtually all regions. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/FSKkBrcj58 https://t.co/dyk1gwJxMt

.@SecBlinken: As we take concrete actions to reduce emissions and prepare for the unavoidable impacts of climate change, we have an opportunity. We’re rooting for every country, every business, every community around the world to succeed in this effort. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/obtpPiBDqa

The U.S. is committed to working hand in hand with international leaders, civil society, businesses, and communities and getting countries around the world to step up and meet the climate challenge. Learn how: https://t.co/B0xyt6jFWB. #LeadersClimateSummit
The 10 warmest years on record have occurred since 2005. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. https://t.co/FSKkBrcj58 #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/r06Iqlf3Mb

During today’s telephonic press briefing, @StateDeputySpox discussed @SecBlinken’s participation in a virtual C5+1 Ministerial with Central Asian countries & announced that Jeffrey Feltman will serve as the U.S. Special Envoy for the Horn of Africa. More: https://t.co/lFEc1cPkYa.

.@SecBlinken: We’ll put the climate crisis at the center of our foreign policy and national security, as @POTUS instructed us to do in his first week in office. Climate is not a trading card – it’s our future. https://t.co/ja7TYadjOw
The 2020 wildfire season burned more than 10 million acres. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. https://t.co/FSKkBrcj58 #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/pRAPeIBZfF

.@SecBlinken: We will significantly increase our investment in clean energy research and development, because it’s how we will catalyze breakthroughs that benefit American communities and create American jobs. https://t.co/HTFZRtwn7P

.@SecBlinken: No matter what country we’re from, we know the world that we want to pass on to our children and our grandchildren. I can think of no better or more urgent cause to bring us together. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/Vn44snbEQK

The United States will lead from a position of strength to address the climate crisis, seizing every chance we get to raise climate issues with our allies and partners, including at the G7 and at next month’s ministerial meeting of the @ArcticCouncil. https://t.co/IyBQflyCkF
Natural disasters in 2020 cost the United States around $100 billion. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/v7eOm5b191 https://t.co/MnnumZE4nR
Tune into Session 5 of the #LeadersClimateSummit on “The Economic Opportunities of Climate Action” with @POTUS, @SecretaryPete, @ambassadortai, and @ginamccarthy46. https://t.co/skk7TEveQm
Live now! @secgranholm, @secraimondo, and @ClimateEnvoy Kerry kick off day two of the #LeadersClimateSummit with Session 4 on “Unleashing Climate Innovation.” https://t.co/QtCzDOrEX6
Good morning! Join us for day two of the #LeadersClimateSummit. Watch @secgranholm, @secraimondo, and @ClimateEnvoy host Session 4 on “Unleashing Climate Innovation.” https://t.co/f475ZuUccH

.@SecBlinken: If we work together, we can do more than just address this crisis. We can turn it into an opportunity to improve our societies and deliver for people worldwide, and we can lay the foundation for cooperation on other shared challenges. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/GmCyBMMouF

2020 was the second-warmest year on record based on @NOAA’s temperature data. Land areas were hottest on record. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/v7eOm4TpKr https://t.co/fOX6hdpBwF
.@SecBlinken: Today, the science is unequivocal: We need to keep the Earth’s warming to 1.5 degrees Celsius to avoid catastrophe. https://t.co/FdZlStF1zV

From 1900 to 1980, a new temperature record was set on average every 13.5 years; from 1981–2019, a new record was set every 3 years. Now is the time to #RestoreOurEarth. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/v7eOm4TpKr https://t.co/dhdfiHuSbr

.@SecBinken: No country can overcome this existential threat alone. We’re in this together. And what each of our nations does or does not do will not only impact people of our own country, but people everywhere. #LeadersClimateSummit https://t.co/4a9hTDR4w4

We must be bold, do more to reduce emissions, and #RestoreOurEarth for generations to come. To learn more about our efforts to address the climate crisis, tune in Friday for day 2 of the #LeadersClimateSummit. https://t.co/v7eOm5b191 https://t.co/qcTFstDzVB
